title: 5 Roles Every Growing Company Should Automate First
excerpt: Not sure where to start with AI automation? These five roles consistently deliver the highest ROI when replaced with AI employees.
author: Simon Insinger
content:
## Start where the ROI is highest

Every company considering AI automation asks the same question: where do we start?

After building dozens of AI employees across industries, we've identified five roles that consistently deliver the fastest payback and most significant operational impact.

## 1. Executive Assistant

**Why automate**: EAs spend 70-80% of their time on schedulable, pattern-based tasks. Calendar management, email triage, meeting prep, and travel booking are all highly automatable.

**Typical savings**: $60K-90K/year in salary + benefits

**Time to ROI**: 30 days

The AI EA is our most popular build for a reason — it delivers immediate, visible value to the person who usually makes buying decisions: the founder or CEO.

## 2. Sales Development Representative (SDR)

**Why automate**: SDR work is fundamentally about volume and personalization. AI can research prospects, craft personalized outreach, and manage follow-up sequences at a scale no human can match.

**Typical savings**: $65K-85K/year per SDR replaced

**Time to ROI**: 45 days

The key insight: AI SDRs don't just save money — they increase pipeline. Most clients see 2-3x more qualified meetings within the first quarter.

## 3. Research Analyst

**Why automate**: Research is reading, synthesizing, and summarizing. AI processes information orders of magnitude faster than humans, pulling from dozens of sources simultaneously.

**Typical savings**: $70K-100K/year per analyst replaced

**Time to ROI**: 30 days

Particularly impactful for investment firms, strategy teams, and competitive intelligence functions.

## 4. Customer Support (Tier 1)

**Why automate**: The majority of customer support inquiries follow predictable patterns. AI can resolve 60-80% of tickets instantly, escalating only complex issues to human agents.

**Typical savings**: $40K-55K/year per agent replaced

**Time to ROI**: 14 days

The fastest path to ROI on this list. Customer satisfaction often improves because response times drop from hours to seconds.

## 5. Data Entry & Operations Coordinator

**Why automate**: This is the most obviously automatable role, yet many companies still employ humans for data entry, CRM updates, and cross-system coordination.

**Typical savings**: $45K-60K/year

**Time to ROI**: 14 days

Eliminates human error in data processing and frees up your operations team for strategic work.

## How to prioritize

We recommend starting with the role that:

1. Costs the most relative to the work produced
2. Has the most clearly defined, repeatable workflows
3. Would benefit most from 24/7 availability
4. The founder/CEO personally feels the pain of

Usually, that's the Executive Assistant. But every business is different.

## The compounding effect

Here's what most companies discover: once the first AI employee is running, you spot automation opportunities everywhere. The second build goes faster. The third even faster.

Before long, you've built an AI operations layer that runs 24/7, costs a fraction of a traditional team, and continuously improves.

That's not just automation. That's competitive advantage.
